perturbation.original_text: AT&T shall pay Vendor no later than sixty (60) days after receipt of such monthly payment request for Structured Payments. AT&T shall pay, or provide a written notice of any good faith dispute to, Vendor no later than ninety (90) days after receipt of an invoice for Maintenance Fees. Any dispute of an invoice for Maintenance Fees shall be made by AT&T in good faith and shall set forth in writing a detailed description of the disputed amounts and the reason for such dispute.
perturbation.changed_text: AT&T shall pay Vendor no later than one hundred and twenty (120) days after receipt of such monthly payment request for Structured Payments. AT&T shall pay, or provide a written notice of any good faith dispute to, Vendor no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of an invoice for Maintenance Fees. Any dispute of an invoice for Maintenance Fees shall be made by AT&T in good faith and shall set forth in writing a detailed description of the disputed amounts and the reason for such dispute.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadlines for Structured Payments and Maintenance Fees. Originally, AT&T had 60 days to pay Structured Payments and 90 days (or provide a dispute notice) for Maintenance Fees. The modified text gives AT&T 120 days for Structured Payments but only 30 days for Maintenance Fees. This contradicts the previous expectation of longer payment terms for Maintenance Fees, causing uncertainty in enforcement, potentially leading to disputes over which deadline applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Promptly after Location Acceptance, Vendor will render an invoice in duplicate to AT&T with respect to the applicable Cell Site. Such invoice for such Cell Site shall be consistent with the AT&T purchase order and detail the Material, Deliverables and Services provided for such Cell Site. The Vendor invoice must specify in detail, if applicable, (i) quantities of each item of Material, Deliverable, and Services, (ii) prices of each item of Material, Deliverable, and Services, (iii) whether the item is taxable and the amount of tax per item, (iv) total amounts for each item, (v) total amount of applicable sales or use taxes, (vi) discounts, if any, (vii) shipping charges, if any, (viii) total amount due, (ix) remit to address, (x) description of Material, Deliverables, and Services, and (xi) special service charges, if any. Each invoice shall specifically reference that the invoice will be paid in accordance with the terms set forth in this Agreement and all payments for amounts due on such invoice for Material, Deliverables, and Services provided under the Build Addendum will be made in accordance with the Structured Payments Schedule attached as Schedule 3 to the Build Addendum. AT&T will promptly review such invoice and, unless AT&T disputes in good faith the amounts set forth therein, will promptly acknowledge that the Structured Payment set forth therein is due in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Build Addendum. In the event that AT&T disputes in good faith any amounts set forth in any such invoice, AT&T shall give written notice of such disputed amounts to Vendor prior to the due date of the first Structure Payment set forth in such invoice. Vendor will provide a payment request to AT&T on a monthly basis on the first day of each calendar month for the Structured Payments set forth in the Build Addendum and the Maintenance Fees set forth in the Maintenance Addendum, in each case as provided in the respective addendum. AT&T shall pay Vendor no later than one hundred and twenty (120) days after receipt of such monthly payment request for Structured Payments. AT&T shall pay, or provide a written notice of any good faith dispute to, Vendor no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of an invoice for Maintenance Fees. Any dispute of an invoice for Maintenance Fees shall be made by AT&T in good faith and shall set forth in writing a detailed description of the disputed amounts and the reason for such dispute. Notwithstanding any good faith dispute, AT&T shall pay all undisputed amounts in full to Vendor no later than the due date for such invoice. Invoices received by AT&T more than one (1) year after the Delivery of Material and Deliverables or the provision of Services are untimely and AT&T has no obligation to pay such invoices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies payment deadlines, creating a direct inconsistency. The original text had AT&T paying Structured Payments within 60 days and Maintenance Fees within 90 days (or dispute notice). The changed text alters this to 120 days for Structured Payments and only 30 days for Maintenance Fees, contradicting the prior expectation of longer payment terms for Maintenance Fees. The contradicted text also explicitly states 'AT&T shall pay Vendor no later than one hundred and twenty (120) days after receipt of such monthly payment request for Structured Payments. AT&T shall pay, or provide a written notice of any good faith dispute to, Vendor no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of an invoice for Maintenance Fees' confirming this contradiction. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Prior to exercising its right to offset amounts pursuant to this Section 3.22, AT&T shall provide written notice to Vendor of its intention to offset such amounts and to the extent any dispute between the Parties exists in respect of the amount to be offset, AT&T must first utilize the Escalation Procedure in Section 4.8(a).
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to exercising its right to offset amounts pursuant to this Section 3.22, AT&T does not need to provide written notice to Vendor of its intention to offset such amounts. Any dispute between the Parties exists in respect of the amount to be offset, AT&T is not required utilize the Escalation Procedure in Section 4.8(a).
perturbation.explanation: The changed text directly contradicts the original clause regarding AT&T's offset right. Initially, AT&T was required to provide written notice and utilize an escalation procedure before offsetting amounts. The modification removes both of these requirements. This creates uncertainty regarding the proper procedure for AT&T to exercise its offset right. The original clause ensured Vendor's awareness and an opportunity to resolve disputes. The change provides AT&T no obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any amounts payable (including any recoupment claims or other amounts to be reimbursed) by Vendor or its Affiliates to AT&T or its Affiliates under this Agreement (including under the Build Addendum or the Maintenance Addendum) may be offset against the Maintenance Fees or any payment due or to become due to Vendor or its Affiliates from AT&T or its Affiliates under the Transport Agreement, Master License Agreement; provided, however, that AT&T's offset right under this Section 3.22 shall not apply to any Structured Payments for any Cell Site that has reached Location Acceptance and AT&T hereby waives all such rights of setoff and recoupment against such Structured Payments. Prior to exercising its right to offset amounts pursuant to this Section 3.22, AT&T does not need to provide written notice to Vendor of its intention to offset such amounts. Any dispute between the Parties exists in respect of the amount to be offset, AT&T is not required utilize the Escalation Procedure in Section 4.8(a).
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original requirement for AT&T to provide written notice and utilize an escalation procedure before offsetting amounts. The contradicted text contains the modified text, effectively eliminating AT&T's obligations to provide notice and utilize the escalation procedure which creates a clear inconsistency in the agreement regarding the process for AT&T to exercise its offset rights and constitutes an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: AT&T may have inspectors at the Cell Sites to inspect the performance and quality of the Work and to ensure Vendor's compliance with the plans and Specifications and with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Any AT&T inspectors, employees or agents, however, shall have no authority to direct or advise Vendor concerning the method or manner by which the Work is to be performed.
perturbation.changed_text: AT&T may have inspectors at the Cell Sites to inspect the performance and quality of the Work and to ensure Vendor's compliance with the plans and Specifications and with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Any AT&T inspectors, employees or agents, shall have the authority to direct or advise Vendor concerning the method or manner by which the Work is to be performed.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates a direct contradiction regarding the authority of AT&T inspectors. Originally, AT&T inspectors had no authority to direct or advise the Vendor on how to perform the work, only to ensure compliance. The modification gives the inspectors full authority to provide direction and advice. This creates conflict concerning the level of control AT&T has over the Vendor's work and the extent to which the Vendor maintains its independence. It undermines the 'Independent Contractor' clause, as the change in text allow AT&T inspector, to guide the Vendor on how to do the work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AT&T may have inspectors at the Cell Sites to inspect the performance and quality of the Work and to ensure Vendor's compliance with the plans and Specifications and with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Any AT&T inspectors, employees or agents, shall have the authority to direct or advise Vendor concerning the method or manner by which the Work is to be performed. Vendor has sole authority, responsibility and control over the Work and shall exercise its full responsibilities as an independent contractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text grants AT&T inspectors the authority to direct the Vendor, while the contradicted text emphasizes the Vendor's sole authority and independent contractor status. This creates a direct conflict, as the Vendor cannot simultaneously have sole authority and be subject to direction from AT&T inspectors. This directly breaks the Vendor's defined independence. The changed text says 'AT&T inspectors... shall have the authority to direct or advise Vendor', while the contradicted text says 'Vendor has sole authority, responsibility and control over the Work and shall exercise its full responsibilities as an independent contractor.'
